threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train' and look puzzled when someone mentions the overground.
Last night on the 333, a guy was reciting Shakespeare to a cat on his lap. The cat looked more interested than most of us.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I stumbled onto a spontaneous rooftop concert while getting my döner at midnight. That's when I knew I was truly part of this chaotic charm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find secret gardens behind unassuming apartment blocks, where neighbors gather for spontaneous barbecues and summer soirées.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the taco truck parked outside by the type of salsa it serves—especially if it’s the good green one.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 late night, driver held up by a street performer having a solo rave. Only in SF can a transit delay turn into a dance party.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all Gaudí, but the true heart beats in the little plazas where locals sip vermut and gossip about last night’s football.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by their hesitant smile when ordering a coffee; locals just know to skip the 'por favor' and get straight to the point.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think paying for the bus is a novelty instead of a soul-crushing necessity. Locals know, it’s just part of the grind.
Last week on the 35, a lad started singing the whole of 'Wonderwall' and everyone joined in. Never thought I'd see Brixton turn a bus into a karaoke night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid selling handmade bracelets at the U-Bahn entrance while his grandma bartered for vegetables at the stand next door.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll find spontaneous dance parties in the street when the sun comes out. No permit, just locals vibing together. It's a vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they’ve got a favorite taco truck but still complain about the 14 Mission being a total nightmare at rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 21 bus doing a full karaoke set while the driver rolled his eyes. Only in the Tenderloin would that feel like normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the mosaics at Park Güell are the pinnacle of art in Gràcia, but they miss the real gems—local murals tucked away in side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their bewilderment over the tram schedules—those shiny maps don’t help when the trams have a mind of their own.
